CPC Designer

Use the workflow in the left sidebar (Upload → Set CPC count → Run) to create or modify the network. The full network details will appear in the main dashboard view after running.
How to use CPC Designer

Step 1 — Upload base file: Click "Upload base file" in the sidebar dropdown, drop your Excel with the standard 15-column format. The module parses lat/long, classifies verticals into 3 buckets, and prepares the dataset.

Step 2 — Set CPC count: Pick how many CPCs to create (6-10). More CPCs = shorter rider trips but more facilities.

Step 3 — Run Designer: The module clusters all pickup points using k-means, names each CPC by area, computes feeder distances, and assigns riders. The full dashboard becomes available with map, schedules, and all configuration.

After running, you can flex k, speed, capacity, cutoffs, and feeder waves directly on the dashboard without revisiting this workflow.

Feeder Routes

Wave-by-wave fleet planning per CPC — auto-suggests vehicle types based on load, flags spillover, and lets you add waves where coverage is short.
SELECTChoose CPC & bucket
FLEETVehicle types & speeds
Auto-selected per wave based on load. Smaller vehicle = lower cost; larger = fewer trips.
Per-fleet feeder speed (CPC → SC)
PLANWave-by-wave fleet plan
Showing fleet assignment for selected CPC + bucket
CPC-specific (NDD only by default)
NETWORKAll-CPC fleet summary
Total feeder fleet required across the network

📂 Upload Base File

Upload an Excel/CSV with the same column format as the NCR base file. The module will re-cluster, name CPCs, and recompute all schedules for your city.
